executive_summary: C-009 composition row: role self-review is one of finding-009's six improvement loops, and today its trigger lives in agent session memory so it silently dies (Adam went 3 weeks unscored 06-14 to 07-03; the coordinator has ZERO durable rubric-score rows ever; Solomon has adherence activity but no score trail). This PRD extracts Adam's existing self-assessment scaffolding (lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs) into a role-agnostic shared core, wires the coordinator and Solomon onto it as their own emitters (additive to their existing duty-parity/adherence probes, not a replacement), adds a gauge-registry entry per role checking latest-score age vs cadence so a lapsed loop surfaces instead of dying silently, and narrowly scopes the existing grade-action-verify contract (lib/fleet/verify-score-contract.mjs) to REFUSE (not just advisory-log) a below-threshold score with empty committed_actions. No new table, scheduler, or alert channel — composition into the ONE existing gauge-registry/gauge-runner/feedback-table spine only.

functional_requirements.title: Extract a role-agnostic self-score core; Adam's writer is byte-identical after the refactor
functional_requirements.description: Create lib/governance/role-self-score.cjs, extracting classifyBelowThreshold, derivePriorOutcomes, generateCommittedActions, overallString, and assembleScore from lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs into pure functions parameterized by a RoleConfig {role, dimensions, scorers, actionHints, target, belowThresholdAt}. Refactor lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs to define ADAM_CONFIG and delegate to the shared core. scripts/adam-self-assessment-writer.cjs, the ADAM_SELF_SCORE_CADENCE gate, and .adam-self-assessment-state.json turn-counter state are UNCHANGED (they live in the writer, not the pure core).
acceptance_criteria: A golden-snapshot test feeds a fixed signals object through both the pre-refactor lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs and the post-refactor shared-core-backed version and asserts byte-identical assembleScore() output
scripts/adam-self-assessment-writer.cjs requires no changes to its own logic (cadence gate, state file, review_key) beyond importing the refactored module

functional_requirements.title: Coordinator emits its own graded self-score on its existing self-review DUE branch
functional_requirements.description: Add a COORDINATOR_CONFIG (dimensions sourced from the already-documented coordinator rubric in .claude/commands/coordinator.md, ~line 839 — do not invent new dimension names) to scripts/coordinator-self-review.mjs. On the DUE branch (delta >= REVIEW_EVERY), assemble a score via the shared core and insert a feedback row with category='coordinator_self_assessment' (dimensions + committed_actions + prior_action_outcomes). This is a NEW category distinct from the existing category='coordinator_review' (raw captured worker-feedback text) — update the TRI_PARTY_VERIFY_V1 read path to read the new graded category instead of trying (and failing) to parseScore() raw capture rows.
acceptance_criteria: Running coordinator-self-review.mjs against a fixture at/after the DUE threshold inserts exactly one feedback row with category=coordinator_self_assessment containing populated dimensions and committed_actions
TRI_PARTY_VERIFY_V1 successfully parses a score from the new category (previously always empty against category=coordinator_review)

functional_requirements.title: Solomon emits a rubric self-score, additive to its existing duty-parity adherence probe
functional_requirements.description: Add a SOLOMON_CONFIG (D1-D5 rubric from leo_protocol_sections section_type='solomon_role_contract', the DB source of CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md) and a new scripts/solomon-self-assessment-writer.cjs mirroring adam-self-assessment-writer.cjs's shape, emitting feedback category='solomon_self_assessment'. This is ADDITIVE to (not a replacement for) the existing scripts/solomon-self-adherence-review.mjs, which measures duty PARITY/compliance — a different concept from rubric quality self-scoring. Wire the emission by adding an explicit instruction to Solomon's deep-sweep procedure via a leo_protocol_sections DB edit (section_type='solomon_role_contract') + node scripts/generate-claude-md-from-db.js regeneration — Solomon's deep-sweep tick is agent-prompt-driven (script: null in solomon-startup-check.mjs), not code-driven, so this half of the wiring is a documented instruction, not a function call.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/solomon-self-assessment-writer.cjs, invoked standalone with a fixture signals object, inserts a feedback row with category=solomon_self_assessment containing dimensions + committed_actions + prior_action_outcomes

CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md's deep-sweep section (post-regeneration) explicitly instructs invoking the writer at the end of each deep-sweep, and explicitly distinguishes this from the existing duty-parity adherence probe
solomon-self-adherence-review.mjs is untouched (still emits category=solomon_adherence_drift unchanged)

functional_requirements.title: Gauge-registry entry per role: latest-score age vs cadence surfaces a lapsed loop
functional_requirements.description: Add 3 entries to lib/governance/gauge-registry.js (adam_self_score_age, coordinator_self_score_age, solomon_self_score_age), each with a distinct detectorFn key. Add ONE shared factory in scripts/gauge-runner.mjs's buildDetectorResolvers map — e.g. staleSelfScoreDetector(category, cadenceHours) — that queries the latest feedback row for the given category, computes its age, and trips (tripWhen) when age exceeds cadence. 3 distinct keys each delegate to this one factory (the detectorFn map is key->fn, so 'one shared resolver' means one shared implementation behind 3 keys, per the existing stub-then-two-additive-edits adoption contract).
acceptance_criteria: A fixture with no coordinator_self_assessment feedback row (or one older than cadence) causes node scripts/gauge-runner.mjs to log GAUGE coordinator_self_score_age=true and insert a feedback row with category=invariant_gauge_finding
A fixture with a fresh (within-cadence) score row does NOT trip the gauge for that role
gauge-runner-liveness.js's 2h staleness check and MAX_DETECTORS_PER_RUN cap are unaffected (verified against current values, well under the 50-detector limit)

functional_requirements.title: Emitter refuses an invalid score row, narrowly scoped (never refuses inconclusive-only or escalation-eligible cycles)
functional_requirements.description: Wire lib/fleet/verify-score-contract.mjs's validateScoreContract() into the shared core's write path (lib/governance/role-self-score.cjs) so a payload violating Rule 1 (below-threshold dimension + empty committed_actions) or Rule 2 (missing prior_action_outcomes when the prior cycle had committed_actions) is REFUSED: log-and-skip (fail-soft, never throw, matching this subsystem's fail-open ethos elsewhere) with NO feedback row inserted. This applies uniformly to Adam, coordinator, and Solomon emitters (retrofitting Adam's current advisory-only annotate-and-still-write behavior). Per risk-agent mitigation: NEVER refuse when the contract verdict is inconclusive (valid===null, e.g. all-dimensions-inconclusive cycles) or when the cycle is escalation-eligible (streak>=3) but otherwise contract-valid — those rows are the ones that most need recording and must always write.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture payload with a below-threshold dimension and committed_actions=[] is refused: no feedback row inserted, a SUPPRESSED/REFUSED reason is logged naming the failing rule
A fixture payload representing an escalation-eligible cycle (streak>=3) with populated committed_actions still WRITES its feedback row (does not regress from 'some data' to 'no data')
A fixture payload where all 8 (or role-equivalent) dimensions are inconclusive still WRITES its feedback row (never refused for lack of signal)

technical_requirements.title: No new table, scheduler, or alert channel
technical_requirements.description: All emission uses the existing feedback table category convention; all staleness detection uses the existing gauge-registry.js array + gauge-runner.mjs execution surface; no cron/scheduler is added anywhere.

technical_requirements.title: CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md is DB-generated — never hand-edit
technical_requirements.description: Edit leo_protocol_sections rows where section_type='solomon_role_contract', then regenerate via node scripts/generate-claude-md-from-db.js. .claude/commands/coordinator.md is read-only reference for dimension names in this PRD (not edited) — coordinator wiring is entirely inside coordinator-self-review.mjs.

technical_requirements.title: Adam's refactor is regression-protected by a golden-snapshot test
technical_requirements.description: The FR-1 extraction must not change any observable Adam behavior — enforced by a byte-identical pre/post assembleScore() comparison test, run before the PR merges.

test_scenarios.scenario: Golden-snapshot: Adam's assembleScore is byte-identical pre/post shared-core refactor
test_scenarios.expected: Deep-equal output for a fixed signals fixture
One feedback row, category=coordinator_self_assessment, with dimensions+committed_actions

test_scenarios.scenario: Gauge trips on stale/missing score row
test_scenarios.expected: detector returns tripped=true; gauge-runner inserts invariant_gauge_finding

test_scenarios.scenario: Gauge does NOT trip on fresh score row
test_scenarios.expected: detector returns tripped=false; no finding inserted
Write refused, no row inserted, reason logged

test_scenarios.scenario: Non-refusal: escalation-eligible cycle with committed_actions still writes

acceptance_criteria: Stop a role's tick for N cadences in a test fixture (no/stale feedback row for that category) — the corresponding gauge-registry entry flags the lapse (FR-4)
Emit a below-threshold score with no committed_actions through the shared writer core — it is refused, no row inserted (FR-5)
Adam's existing self-assessment behavior is unchanged (golden-snapshot proof, FR-1)
Coordinator and Solomon each gain their own durable graded score trail where today they have none or only adjacent adherence-probe data (FR-2, FR-3)

risks.risk: Refuse-at-write silently drops legitimate low-signal cycles, regressing from 'some data' to 'no data' for Adam (5/8 dimensions are routinely inconclusive; escalation cycles are the ones most needing a durable row)
risks.mitigation: Refusal scoped strictly to Rule-1/Rule-2 contract violations; explicit tests (TS-7, TS-8) prove escalation-eligible and all-inconclusive cycles still write

risks.risk: New Solomon self-score channel is mistaken for / duplicates the existing solomon-self-adherence-review.mjs duty-parity probe
risks.mitigation: PRD and the CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md deep-sweep instruction explicitly name both channels and state the distinction (adherence=duty compliance, self-assessment=rubric quality score); solomon-self-adherence-review.mjs is untouched

risks.risk: Hand-editing CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md directly instead of via leo_protocol_sections DB gets silently reverted on the next generate-claude-md-from-db.js run
risks.mitigation: TR-2 mandates the DB-section edit path; verified against generate-claude-md-from-db.js section-file-mapping before implementation

risks.risk: Coordinator's new category=coordinator_self_assessment breaks the existing TRI_PARTY_VERIFY_V1 read path if not updated in the same change
risks.mitigation: FR-2 explicitly includes updating the read path in the same PR, with TS-2 proving the new category parses

dependencies: lib/fleet/verify-score-contract.mjs (existing, unmodified logic, new call site)
data_contracts: feedback table: 2 new category values (coordinator_self_assessment, solomon_self_assessment), same JSON shape as the existing adam_self_assessment convention — no schema/migration change
observability_rollout: Gauge trips surface as feedback rows (category=invariant_gauge_finding) — same existing surface as all other gauges, visible via the coordinator inbox/dashboard

system_architecture.summary: A role-agnostic self-score core (extracted from Adam's existing implementation) is reused by three per-role emitters; a shared gauge-runner resolver checks each role's latest-score age; the existing grade-action-verify contract gates all three emitters at write time.

components.name: lib/governance/role-self-score.cjs
components.change: NEW — pure scaffolding extracted from lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs

components.name: lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs
components.change: Refactored to delegate to the shared core via ADAM_CONFIG (behavior-identical)

components.name: scripts/coordinator-self-review.mjs
components.change: DUE branch now emits a graded coordinator_self_assessment row; TRI_PARTY_VERIFY_V1 read path updated

components.name: scripts/gauge-runner.mjs
components.change: 1 new shared detector factory (staleSelfScoreDetector) behind 3 registry keys

components.name: lib/fleet/verify-score-contract.mjs
components.change: Unmodified — new call site in the shared core's write path applies refuse-at-write

components.name: leo_protocol_sections (section_type=solomon_role_contract)
components.change: Adds the deep-sweep self-score-emission instruction, regenerates CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md

implementation_approach.summary: Extract-and-reuse, not rebuild: generalize Adam's existing, already-shipped emitter into a shared core, then wire coordinator (code) and Solomon (prompt instruction + new small writer) onto it, add the gauge entries, and narrow-scope the refusal enforcement with explicit non-regression tests.
steps: 1. Extract lib/governance/role-self-score.cjs from lib/adam/self-assessment.cjs; refactor Adam's module to use it via ADAM_CONFIG; add the golden-snapshot regression test (TS-1) FIRST to lock in Adam's current behavior before touching anything else.

2. Define COORDINATOR_CONFIG (dimensions from coordinator.md) and SOLOMON_CONFIG (dimensions from leo_protocol_sections solomon_role_contract); wire coordinator-self-review.mjs's DUE branch and a new solomon-self-assessment-writer.cjs onto the shared core.
3. Add the refuse-at-write call (validateScoreContract) inside the shared core's write path, scoped per FR-5, with TS-6/TS-7/TS-8 proving the scope boundary.
4. Add the 3 gauge-registry entries + the shared staleSelfScoreDetector factory in gauge-runner.mjs; verify against gauge-runner-liveness.js's existing staleness check.
5. Update TRI_PARTY_VERIFY_V1's read path in coordinator-self-review.mjs to the new category.
6. Edit leo_protocol_sections (solomon_role_contract) to add the deep-sweep emission instruction; regenerate CLAUDE_SOLOMON.md.
7. Run the full test suite (unit + the golden-snapshot regression) and the 2 e2e acceptance scenarios from the SD description.

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Seed a fixture with no coordinator_self_assessment feedback row, run node scripts/gauge-runner.mjs
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: Console shows GAUGE coordinator_self_score_age=true and a new feedback row category=invariant_gauge_finding appears

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Call the shared writer core with a below-threshold dimension and committed_actions=[]
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: The call is refused; no feedback row is written; the refusal reason names the failing grade-action-verify rule

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run node scripts/coordinator-self-review.mjs in DUE mode against a fixture at/after the review-every threshold
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: A new feedback row (category=coordinator_self_assessment) appears with populated dimensions + committed_actions
